new_classes.definition_en: A quality borne by a sparkling wine indicating that it was produced in the French province of Champagne exclusively from grapes harvested in that province.

A wine production process carried out in the French province of Champagne using exclusively grapes harvested in that province, resulting in a sparkling wine classified as Champagne under CN 2204 10 11.
A sparkling wine produced in the French province of Champagne from grapes exclusively harvested in that province, classified under CN subheading 2204 10 11.

coverage_explanation: The three defining criteria from the legal text are captured: (1) the product is a sparkling wine (SparklingWineQuality restriction), (2) it is produced in the French province of Champagne (ChampagneRegionOriginQuality quality restriction), and (3) it is made exclusively from grapes harvested in that province (encoded in the ChampagneSparklingWineProduction process restriction and the origin quality). The minor gap (score 0.95 rather than 1.0) is that the 'exclusively' grapes condition and the precise geographic boundary of the province of Champagne cannot be formally enforced within OWL-DL without a more granular geographic ontology or additional PDO/GI individuals that are not yet present in the TBox. The parent class WineFreshGrapesGrapeMust2204 provides the correct heading-level ancestry (CN 2204), and the sparkling wine quality aligns with the 2204 10 subheading level.
